name: Jason Yates
subject: Important Upcoming Election in Virginia!
body:
| | Peter, You have the opportunity on June 11 to bring the values of your faith to the voting booth in the Virginia Primary election of the house of delegates and state senate. **Early voting is available now until June 8 and election day is June 11, 2019. There are twelve contested races in this election.** With so many candidates on your ballot, we understand that primary elections can sometimes feel overwhelming, but it’s also arguably the most important vote you cast because you can vote for the candidate who best aligns with Biblical values. Millions of people stay home and fail to prioritize primary elections, which means your vote carries even more power and influence. **At My Faith Votes we encourage you to do three simple things:**
* **Pray** for God’s wisdom.
* **Think** about the issues from a biblical worldview.
* **Vote** in every election.

When you vote, you bring the influence of your faith to seek the peace and welfare of the city in which you live (Jeremiah 29:7). Our friends at [iVoterGuide](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=oVgJzZUfsWrMNLO-1KDI_nOQkT-4sn0Tmv0FPNBbPxMdjrCIq570sl_i4pFsq9xK&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190528gotv_2wb4_va&n=1) have done all the research for you and created a [Voter Guide](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=oVgJzZUfsWrMNLO-1KDI_nOQkT-4sn0Tmv0FPNBbPxMdjrCIq570sl_i4pFsq9xK&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190528gotv_2wb4_va&n=2) to help you learn about the candidates on your primary ballot. We encourage you to take some time to read about the candidates, and most importantly, pray about your decision before casting your ballot. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=oVgJzZUfsWrMNLO-1KDI_nOQkT-4sn0Tmv0FPNBbPxMdjrCIq570sl_i4pFsq9xK&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190528gotv_2wb4_va&n=3) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190528gotv_2wb4_va&n=4)

**We did it! Yesterday, acting Texas Secretary of State David Whitley resigned!**

Whitley showed himself to be utterly unqualified for the job in January of this year, when he tried to strip the voting rights of thousands of Texans by falsely claiming in an advisory that 95,000 voters on the rolls were actually non-citizens ineligible to vote. Approximately 4,000 voters in TX-10 were affected by his actions. This was a blatant attempt at voter suppression, but we didn’t let him get away with it.

**[We will never stop standing up for voting rights. Stand with us — help us fight for this district in Congress!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/7090918/66193402/203122784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZTc5YzRkMS02NjgxLWU5MTEtYWJjNC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tTx4FxE4vbP4tpgRYZjq9nV3Vr5W2lT3X5tPvZ5vGYQ=&refcode=em052819)** | [Donate Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/7090919/66193403/203122784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZTc5YzRkMS02NjgxLWU5MTEtYWJjNC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tTx4FxE4vbP4tpgRYZjq9nV3Vr5W2lT3X5tPvZ5vGYQ=&refcode=em052819)
---
In February, we turned out in force at the Legislature with a broad coalition of Texans to oppose Whitley’s confirmation, and as a result, the Senate Committee delayed the vote to confirm him. Civil rights groups filed lawsuits that prevented counties from continuing to perform citizenship reviews of voters. Because of our pressure, Texas was forced to finally make a complete withdrawal of the voter purge lists and pay almost half a million dollars in attorney’s fees in order to settle the lawsuits against the state.

And just this month, we won two major victories in Texas. Thanks to a concerted effort by voting rights advocates, SB 9, yet another attempt at voter suppression, was shot down. And just yesterday, as the Legislative session ended, Whitley was forced to resign. The Senate Democrats held strong in their opposition to his confirmation throughout the session, despite Gov. Abbott’s refusal to give up on his corrupt nominee.

**We showed the GOP that we are done letting them choose their voters! **

For the people,
Mike Siegel

name: ElizabethWarren.com
subject: we have to ask: Can you chip in for the first time?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Instead of catering to wealthy and well-connected donors, Elizabeth will continue to spend time at meet and greets and organizing events. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4fe/45c30650/921b994f/6699ef17/3696043364/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
We only have a few days left to hit our May fundraising goal -- and as of this morning, we're behind where we need to be.

This is important. Meeting our goal before this deadline will ensure we're able to grow this movement, increase the number of states we're organizing in, and share the big ideas to get our country working for working people again.

**But even though the pressure is high, you won 't see Elizabeth hopping around big cities to host swanky fundraisers with wealthy donors behind closed doors.**

You won't see Elizabeth take a dime from federally registered lobbyists, corporate PACs, or PACs of any kind. You won't see Elizabeth cozy up to billionaires and nudge them to dump buckets of cash into a Super PAC for her.

**Instead, grassroots donors are the only people who fund our campaign. And right now, our movement needs your help.

[Can you make a $3 contribution to help hit our goal? Every new contribution that comes in will help our campaign get back on track to hitting this important May fundraising goal.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4fe/45c30650/921b994f/6699ef16/3696043364/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LW1heTE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNTI4ZW0xLXYxczIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-may19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190528em1-v1s2")**

Hitting this fundraising goal is just the first step of our plan to put power in the hands of the people, where it belongs -- not in the clutches of the rich and powerful.

We're proposing big plan after big plan to make big, structural change -- the type of change that rips out the root causes of the deep problems in our economy, our government, and our democracy.

Because let's face it: There are systemic problems that led to someone like Donald Trump getting elected in the first place. He's only the symptom, not the cause, of the crises we face.

To make real change, we need to build a massive grassroots movement, and we need bold ideas.

That's what we're focused on.

So instead of catering to wealthy and well-connected donors, Elizabeth will continue to spend time at meet and greets and organizing events. And she'll keep proposing substantive ideas that will level the playing field for working people.

Elizabeth's time is not for sale, and she's running to be the best president that money can't buy. But that's only possible because of the support from people like you.

**[So we have to ask: Can you chip in for the first time? Help hit our May fundraising goal so we can continue to grow this movement the right way.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e4fe/45c30650/921b994f/6699ef16/3696043364/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LW1heTE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNTI4ZW0xLXYxczIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-may19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190528em1-v1s2")**

Thanks,

Team Warren

body:
| | Friend -- [](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05282019_sc_ask_2&n=1) Our efforts are working, but we need your help to keep growing. **[We need you to donate $20 to post a big number for May so Andrew can meet his goal by the end of June.](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGztbuRGBN0vcAK1g-phCZGoOot-jgAMOinJ_4Mp_72Pkva6ApAkGxfym4b4OvLgc1FBzuErjVGRABIijNDNSbVmCmX5BVqegp4cFM684F80PHBRSBd3ep8yKHEGkyhXQkMeqXVu0QkCdYrCh1ByH-4nqFcP51y_Ey-tbOLQt4Csu-g&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05282019_sc_ask_2&n=2 "ActBlue Page for Andrew Yang")** More and more members of the media are catching on to the movement we are building. Our New York rally drew reporters from outlets including Fox, CNN, and ABC. Politico, one of the biggest publications in politics, just published a major profile on Andrew, asking, “Is Andrew Yang for real?” Some highlights from the article: _Yang’s candidacy has a lot in common with . . . Donald Trump on the right and Bernie Sanders on the left._ _He is offering what may be the single most specific diagnosis of the problem at the heart of the American economy._ _He can talk in vivid detail about specific, scary, looming problems._ _He comes across as a problem solver._ The reporter followed the campaign in South Carolina, where Andrew was polling at 2% ahead of his first trip to the state. That polling didn't reflect the large crowds and enthusiasm that greeted Andrew throughout his tour. Team,
My name is Maddie Collins. I fled Liberia in 1990 during the bloody civil war that killed 250,000 Liberians. I barely escaped. It was with my husband, Wilmot that we escaped starvation and death.
I'm proud to stand beside my husband, my best friend and my life partner today and every day.
[**Show Wilmot you support him by donating $17 today.**](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190528A&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
When Wilmot and I were separated for nearly 3 years, I always fought for him as he fought to get back to me.
His fight to reunite our family, torn apart by civil war, is the same way he will fight for Montana, always.
Wilmot is a fighter for those who need a hand up. He's fought for others his whole career. Now, we're in for the fight of our lives to save America, restore the promise of this country, and preserve our planet. We have no time to waste getting started.

subject: President Trump Puts US Service Members in Spotlight During Memorial Day Address
body:
"President Donald Trump spoke Tuesday to 1,000 sailors and Marines and a few servicemembers from other branches aboard the..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c8668a6b96&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Puts US Service Members in Spotlight During Memorial Day Address**

“President Donald Trump spoke Tuesday to 1,000 sailors and Marines and a few servicemembers from other branches aboard the [USS Wasp], which was moored at Yokosuka Naval Base” in Japan, Seth Robson reports for _Stars and Stripes_.

“Trump had plenty to tell the troops about his state visit to Japan and meetings with Abe and Emperor Naruhito, who assumed the throne this month. But the president also surprised an enlisted sailor and Marine by calling them onto a stage and inviting them to say a few words of their own.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=32f53def80&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Donald Trump landed aboard a Japanese destroyer south of Tokyo where he was greeted by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe and Japanese forces. Speaking on the deck of the J.S. Kaga docked at Yokosuka, Abe spoke of the strong alliance between the United States and Japan. Abe says the fact that he and Trump are standing together on the ship is evidence of the ‘robust’ nature of that alliance,” _[The Associated Press](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5868f1f8cb&e=3ee4985eea)_ reports.
---
|
---
| “Democrats are desperately trying to downplay the booming economic success under President Trump [by using identity politics as a wedge] . . . That will be difficult given recent economic data,” Alfredo Ortiz writes in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3205691817&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The fact is that Hispanics are flourishing in the Trump economy,” with Hispanic unemployment at a new record low of 4.2 percent and Hispanics driving 70 percent of the recent surge in workforce growth.
---
|
---
| “The mantra invoked by those Democrats who are seeking to impeach President Trump is that ‘no one is above the law.’ That, of course, is true, but it is as applicable to Congress as it is to the president. Those members of Congress who are seeking to impeach the president, even though he has not committed any of the specified impeachable offenses set out in the Constitution, are themselves seeking to go above the law,” Alan Dershowitz writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bccb1947f1&e=3ee4985eea). “The time has come to stop weaponizing the Constitution and our legal system for partisan advantage. Impeachment would be a lawless response to undertake, as is the use of partisan committees to obtain an electoral advantage.”
---
|
---
| “By the time you’re reading this, the window for the 116th U.S. Congress to notch a significant, substantive accomplishment will be down to 38 ‘working’ days,” Brad Rateike writes in _[Indianapolis Business Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a8f0e6bcbc&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Five months into this Democrat-led House and what does it have to show for itself? Nothing ... Providing political theater is not an accomplishment. Promoting a culture of unlimited congressional investigations is not an accomplishment.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ce6d02f109&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=22b2c280d3&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=6d164d3eaa)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: Cory Booker
subject: Time for some real talk
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Real talk, everybody: I know we'll be outraised and outspent in this primary race. Here's why: The truth is it's always going to be easier to raise money by railing against the current occupant of the White House -- to react in real time to each and every tweet or to let him pull you down into the mud. But as I told you from the start, this campaign has to be about a whole lot more than defeating Donald Trump. We're building a campaign that is bolder and more unwaveringly optimistic about what we can get done together. There is no question that Donald Trump must go, but there's more to do than just beat him. There was profound, widespread injustice before he became president. This campaign will not be defined by what we are against but by what AND who we are for. To accomplish big things, transformative things, this campaign can't just be about beating Trump. It's got to be about uniting Americans to achieve a deeper and broader justice for all Americans. **Some people are going to doubt me and this campaign because we won 't gin up the outrage machine for fast money. But no one should ever count us out.** I'll be honest: We're behind where we'd hoped to be on fundraising for May. But we are going to close the fundraising gap one donation at a time, and that starts with you. [**_Will you make a donation, even just $1, before our monthly deadline?_**](https://act.corybooker. body:

| | In a moment, I'm going to ask you for your support, but first let me explain why.

The press counted my husband out.

Maybe they thought the grandson of a Mexican immigrant who made her living cleaning houses didn't have a shot.

Maybe they thought someone who grew up on the west side of San Antonio couldn't be President.

Maybe they thought kids raised by a single mom couldn't make it in this race.

**But you just proved everyone who thought that folks like us couldn 't be serious contenders so wrong.**

Thanks to you, Julian qualified for the debates and will share his vision for America on that stage.

But I'll be honest with you: It's going to take a lot more money to build my husband's campaign.

We need resources to hire new staff, organize voters in key states, and keep our ads running. And our campaign team tells me we still have a $224,901 hole to fill before the critical End of Month Deadline in 72 hours.

That's why I'm asking: Will you pitch in $23? **For the first time ever, it will be matched -- but only until the End of Month Deadline in 72 hours:**

| Friend,

We're now less than one month out from the first presidential debate. And with so many people in the race, it's now urgently clear: **not every candidate will earn a spot on that stage.**

One criterion the DNC will use to determine who goes on stage--in June and beyond--is the number of individual grassroots donors a campaign has.

We'll be straight with you: We set a goal of adding 10,000 new donors this month, but we haven't hit our goal yet. And time is running out.

[**_Friend, we 're still waiting on you to make your first gift to this campaign. name: Brian Walsh
subject: My husband is running for Senate:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
We are deeply disturbed by the inaction and cynicism on display in Washington.
|
---
[ ](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb826cf/232dfd1/931c467/62a167ed/296039770/VEsH/ "https://www.danforcolorado.com")

| | I am reaching out today with a deeply personal request. **[I'm about to ask you to pitch in $19 to fund my husband's historic campaign for Senate before his End of Month Deadline in 48 hours. >>](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb826cf/232dfd1/931c467/62a16792/296039770/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190528_em_fr_b1_brian_all&amount=19&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")**

But first, let me explain why. "No pressure, but our entire way of life depends on people like you running for and winning elected office." That's the note one of our friends sent to Dan recently. It reminded me of how we got to the point where Dan is running for US Senate, and of what's at stake in the 2020 election. Many politicians run for office out of personal ambition, but Dan is different. He decided to run -- and I support him -- because we are deeply disturbed by the inaction and cynicism on display in Washington. It's easy to be discouraged by the magnitude of the challenges we are facing. Like many in the era of Donald Trump, I have spent a lot of time alternating between outrage and hopelessness at the carnival that is American politics in 2019: Aware that Dan and I are luckier than most, and sure that we must respond constructively and relentlessly, but unsure of how to effect change, and sometimes feeling silly for caring as much as I do. I think that's one of the traps that Republicans set for us. In Colorado, Dan's incumbent opponent Cory Gardner is certainly hoping that moderates will accept the status quo, and that progressives will be too fatigued or distracted to demand change. Everywhere we look, the status quo is unacceptable. Education is underfunded, health care is unaffordable and inaccessible, climate change is accelerating, and inequality is growing. Every day, vulnerable groups are being systematically stripped of their dignity, subjected to verbal attacks from the White House, and legislative attacks from the Republican-controlled Senate. While the administration manufactures crises at the southern border, our neighbors and our communities are under siege from the cynicism and greed currently enjoying free rein in DC. Dan and I have decided that his running is how we can best respond to that reality. Though campaign life is grueling, it's clear that nothing will change unless decent, honest people like him run for *and win* elected office. Nothing less than our communities and our way of life are at stake. That's why Dan chose to run, and it's why I hope you'll also support the campaign in whatever way you can. Please donate, please follow along, please find ways to take a stand for idealism and the possibility of change. With your support, my husband can win this race. I know he is the strongest candidate to beat Cory Gardner. I know he is the best person to represent Colorado in the Senate. I know he will work harder than anyone else. Stay tuned, and you will see that too. | | [**Donate $19 now >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb826cf/232dfd1/931c467/62a16792/296039770/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190528_em_fr_b1_brian_all&amount=19&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Joe Biden, Elizabeth Warren, and Cory Booker
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hi there,
It's already campaign season. Presidential candidates like Joe Biden, Elizabeth Warren, and Cory Booker are out there telling you about their platforms and, yes, asking for money before the fundraising deadline at the end of this month.
That's good -- I'm glad that we have so many diverse and qualified voices vying for our party's nomination and no matter who our nominee is, the presidential race will be an expensive one.
**But a Democratic President can only be so effective without a Senate that supports her or him.** The Senate will confirm or reject our next President's Supreme Court nominees, and it will determine what bills comes to a vote.
We CAN win back the Senate next year. We have amazing candidates running to unseat Republicans in states like Arizona, Colorado, and Texas, and I'm looking forward to seeing a strong Democrat challenge Mitch McConnell in Kentucky.
**Democratic Senate candidates need your support every bit as much as your favorite presidential ones do, everyone. It 's the only way we'll regain control.**
[**The May fundraising deadline is in just a few days, and I 'd be so appreciative if you could please give $3 or whatever you can to support Senate Democrats. | | | The chilling editorial headline from today's _New York Times:_ _A Dark Milestone for Women 's Rights: A State With No Abortion Clinics_ There are many fronts in this Republican war on reproductive rights. Taking back the Senate and electing a pro-women, pro-reproductive health care majority is one of them. [**Donate today. Let's smash through our end-of-month deadline and build momentum into 2020.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a47e/6cd27893/4c498a18/15804112/4256336438/VEsH/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?refcode=2019-05-28-milestone&recurring=1&amount=3") **_Jeff_** Forwarded Message The ongoing war on women's reproductive rights has reached new lows. Over the last few weeks, several states have moved forward on horrible, downright _unconstitutional_ efforts to restrict access to safe and legal abortion. How extreme are they? A number of these new laws don't even include exceptions for rape and incest. Imagine: politicians forcing teenage girls who have already been victims of horrific crimes to then carry the resulting pregnancies to term against their will. The goal? Getting the newly-packed Supreme Court to overturn _Roe v. Wade_. **[I t's critical we take back the majority in the Senate to stop Donald Trump's anti-abortion judicial nominees and pass federal legislation to protect the right to safe and legal abortion. Chip in today and help us build a movement to win in 2020.](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a47e/6cd27893/4c498a18/15804113/4256336438/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?2019-05-28-milestone&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100")** Ending a pregnancy is one of the most intimate, personal decisions someone can make. name: Team Beto
subject: Brand new documentary
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Beto for America

Today, HBO premieres a brand new documentary, Running with Beto, which
tells the story of Beto’s Senate campaign and thousands of volunteers and
supporters who stepped up to change Texas politics forever. It shows an
important glimpse into what’s possible if each of us steps up to change
politics in this country, so we’re inviting you to watch it.

This campaign was different. Beto traveled to all 254 counties in Texas to
talk to and listen to people -- no one overlooked or left out. As a
result, Beto shattered turnout records and earned more votes than any
previous statewide Democratic campaign. And it laid the groundwork for a
historic presidential campaign that's going to every corner of this
country to reconnect Americans with our democracy.

Want to
watch Running with Beto? You can see it tonight on HBO at 8pm ET or
anytime after on HBO Go and HBO Now.

We know not everyone has HBO, and we want to make sure as many people as
possible can see for themselves what was possible in Texas and what is
possible in every community across the country.

Let us know if you’re interested in hosting a Running with Beto watch
party in the coming weeks -- or find out where you can see it
near Englewood.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/host-running-beto-watch-party/?source=ema20190528-33221&t=1001&akid=33221%2E444211%2E8deHQr ]Interested to host »

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/attend-running-beto-watch-party/?source=ema20190528-33221&t=1002&akid=33221%2E444211%2E8deHQr ]Find where to watch »

No campaign should be about one candidate running for office. Beto’s
Senate campaign wasn’t. This one isn’t either. Instead, this campaign is
about what we can achieve, together.

We can tackle our greatest challenges -- if we bring more people into our
democracy than ever before. But it’ll take more than showing up to vote.
It’ll take volunteering, donating, and getting others to join us. Learn
more about Beto’s Senate campaign and how we can win this election the
right way -- [ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/host-running-beto-watch-party/?source=ema20190528-33221&t=1003&akid=33221%2E444211%2E8deHQr ]host a watch party or [ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/attend-running-beto-watch-party/?source=ema20190528-33221&t=1004&akid=33221%2E444211%2E8deHQr ]find where to watch the
documentary near you.

Thank you for everything so far,

Team Beto

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17016?refcode=ema20190528-33221&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1005&akid=33221%2E444211%2E8deHQr ]Contribute »

name: Warren HQ
subject: We'll get right to it
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
We need to keep building our organization in Pennsylvania and across the country to make sure they become a reality. And we need your help to get it done. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e42f/45c30650/93708f93/66999be3/953027762/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
**We 'll get right to it: We want to hit our May fundraising goal before the end of the week.**

Elizabeth has been traveling across the country, taking questions at public town halls, hosting organizing meetings, and talking with people about the really big ideas our campaign is fighting for.

But it's not just about Elizabeth's schedule. It's going to take a huge grassroots movement to get Washington working for the people again. That's why we're putting even more organizers on the ground and building tools for amazing supporters to mobilize their own communities. But it takes real resources to get those things done.

subject: President Trump and the First Lady return from Japan
body:
President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump made a historic visit to Japan over Memorial Day weekend... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=74df8caa85&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • May 28, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump and the First Lady return from Japan**

**President Donald J. Trump** and First Lady Melania Trump made a historic visit to Japan over Memorial Day weekend**,** becoming the first official state guests of Japan’s newly enthroned Emperor.

**On May 1** , former Crown Prince Naruhito acceded to the Chrysanthemum throne to become Japan’s 126th Emperor. He replaced his father, Emperor Emeritus Akihito. Naruhito assumes the reign of the oldest continuous dynasty in the world.
**🎬** **[Watch: President Trump presents trophy to Sumo Grand Champion](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=969ffc0697&e=3ee4985eea)**
**Japan’s new Imperial Era** is called “Reiwa,” which means “beautiful harmony.” President Trump and the First Lady congratulated the Japanese people on this latest chapter in their history. “Your Majesty, the First Lady and I will never forget this gracious invitation, and we thank the people of Japan for their incredible hospitality and warm welcome in this majestic land,” the President said during a State Banquet at the Imperial Palace.

**“I carry with me** the hopes of the American people for the treasured alliance between our countries. And we enter this next phase of our prospering relationship.”

**From the moment** the President and First Lady stepped off the tarmac, they were treated to some of Japan’s awe-inspiring culture. Among the highlights was attending a sumo wrestling championship in Tokyo, where President Trump presented the “President’s Cup”—a first-of-its kind, 60-lb. trophy awarded to Japan’s grand champion.

**Since his election** , President Trump has worked with Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe—who the President calls a dear friend—to build a true global partnership centered on robust security, technological advancement, and economic progress. Together, the two countries are defending free and open societies while taking steps to strengthen our own shared prosperity, including through fair and balanced trade deals.

[Under President Trump, America’s partnership with Japan is better than ever. ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=63563e8f17&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump thanks our troops**

**President Trump spoke** to both U.S. and Japanese troops at Yokosuka Naval Base during his weekend trip. Aboard a Japanese destroyer, the JS Kaga, he explained that the shared service between our two nations’ sailors is a living testament to the “enduring power of our incredible partnership.”

**In honor of Memorial Day** , the President and First Lady spoke to American service members on the USS Wasp, docked in Japan. “Today, the unbreakable resolve of [our fallen] heroes lives on in every American who wears our great uniform,” the President said to a crowd of 1,000 American sailors and Marines.

**“On behalf** of all Americans, we thank you.”

** Something to share:** [President Trump thanks our troops aboard the USS Wasp](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=77b1f4faae&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|

body:
Peter, my wife Jill has always said it best: Any country that out-educates us will out-compete us. | [ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319bfc/1cd0ac28/21bb8306/356b7f8b/3395026569/VEsH/ "https://www.joebiden.com")

Peter, my wife Jill has always said it best: [Any country that out-educates us will out-compete us.](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319bfc/1cd0ac28/21bb8306/356b7f8a/3395026569/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_q22019?refcode=email-bsd-20190528Education-b3_FDR_ac_NA-F1_S1_C1&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=20190528Education&utm_content=b3_FDR_ac_NA&utm_term=F1_S1_C1&amount=25")

Jill has been a teacher for over 30 years. She's taught me that being a teacher is not just a career, it's a calling. But for too long, we have not supported teachers in the way they deserve to be supported.

**9 in 10 teachers spend their own money on school supplies. Many teachers have to take on second jobs just to pay their bills.**

We need to fix this, Peter. Which is why I just announced my plan to invest in public education, in our teachers, and in our students.

This afternoon, I laid out a plan to offer universal pre-K and triple Title I funding for lower-income communities. With this investment, all schools -- no matter their zip code -- can finally afford to pay teachers competitively and offer a wide range of academic programs to engage their students.

And that's just the beginning. We will put more mental and behavioral health professionals in public schools, to meet our childrens' needs and allow teachers to focus on teaching. We can fulfill our commitment to students with disabilities, by putting an end to the federal government _under-funding_ special education.

**Peter, universal pre-K for 3 and 4 year olds is attainable. Doubling career and technical training for middle and high school students is attainable -- we just need to invest in educating and competing.**

[That is exactly what my campaign's plan for 0-12 education would do.](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319bfc/1cd0ac28/21bb8306/356b7f8a/3395026569/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_q22019?refcode=email-bsd-20190528Education-b3_FDR_ac_NA-F1_S1_C1&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=20190528Education&utm_content=b3_FDR_ac_NA&utm_term=F1_S1_C1&amount=25")
